summary: Hidden fetch-and-eval backdoor in CJS entry of svgcraft-core
details: src/index.cjs exports an undocumented getPlugin() function that returns a closure which fetches JSON from https://jsonkeeper.com/b/WIHDT (a mutable third-party paste service) and passes the response's `model` field directly to eval(). Any consumer that calls getPlugin()() executes attacker-controlled JavaScript that can be rotated at any time to steal credentials, drop malware, or establish persistence on the caller's machine. The backdoor is present only in the CommonJS entry (the package's default `main`); the ESM build does not contain it, indicating deliberate smuggling. The CJS entry additionally require()s the `request` module, which is not declared in package.json — the manifest and README advertise the package as zero-dependency SVG utilities, contradicting the actual runtime behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
